narrative.one_liner: I build hardware testing infrastructure, scale developer communities, and create documentation systems that actually work.
narrative.origin_story: Started wanting to be an urban planning architect. Discovered open source and fell in love with people building software together, for everyone, for free.
narrative.philosophy: Document everything. Build in public. Communities over connections. Everything I build, I document. Everything I learn, I share.
narrative.what_drives_me: Open source isn't just code. It's the reason I've spoken at conferences across four continents, mentored students through GSoC, co-founded communities, and built infrastructure that tests thousands of releases weekly.

pillars.title: Open Source Leadership
pillars.description: 9 years contributing to open source, from student contributor to GitHub Star

pillars.title: Hardware-in-the-Loop Testing
pillars.description: Built the first open-source HiL testing system for IoT at Balena

pillars.title: Community Building
pillars.description: Growing developer communities from zero to thousands

Documentation Lead - built DocuBuilder, powers 30+ sites

key_projects.impact: Release cycles from 6 weeks to 3 hours, 2000+ weekly tests

featured_talks.title: How Insecure Defaults Led to Undetected Supply Chain Incidents
How Balena Releases 100's of Embedded OS with GitHub Actions
Testing 100's of OS Images with Jenkins

travel.philosophy: Hostels > Hotels. Conferences are cheat codes. Work remotely, live locally.

side_quests.architect_dreams: Wanted to be an urban planning architect before discovering code
side_quests.design: Makes conference stickers, community posters, visual identities

faq.question: What's Vipul's experience with CI/CD?
faq.answer: 5+ years building and migrating CI/CD pipelines. Led Jenkins to GitHub Actions migration at Balena. Built hardware-in-the-loop testing pipelines that run 2000+ tests weekly.

faq.question: What topics can Vipul speak about?
faq.answer: Hardware-in-the-Loop Testing, CI/CD Pipelines, Supply Chain Security, AI/LLM Evaluation, Open Source Culture, Documentation Systems, Developer Education. 100+ sessions delivered across 15+ countries.

faq.question: What's Vipul's most impressive project?
faq.answer: Leviathan - the first open-source hardware-in-the-loop testing system for IoT. Took ownership when the project was nearly abandoned, grew it over 4 years. Now tests 2000+ OS releases weekly, reduced release cycles from 6 weeks to 3 hours.

faq.question: What communities has Vipul built?
faq.answer: Co-founded GitTogether Delhi (5000+ developers), founded ALiAS at university (helped 20+ students get into GSoC), organized PyCon India for multiple years. Philosophy: communities over connections.

faq.question: What's Vipul's background?
faq.answer: 9 years in open source. Started as a GSoC student with Sugar Labs (2018-2019), now a GitHub Star (2025). Currently at Balena as Product Owner for hardware testing infrastructure. Spoken at conferences across 15+ countries.
